title: torilis arvensis var. homoeomorpha
intro: Torilis arvensis var. homoeomorpha, also known as wild chervil or horned poppy, is a variety of the Torilis arvensis plant, which belongs to the carrot family. It has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing anxiety and promoting relaxation, as well as aiding in digestion and relieving menstrual cramps. Torilis arvensis var. homoeomorpha can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by infusing the leaves and stems in hot water or a solvent.
